summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the FUXA v1.2.7 /api/runscript endpoint with test:true compiles and executes arbitrary JavaScript via Module._compile, allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute operating system commands on the server.
expected_security_impact: Sending a POST request to /api/runscript with test:true and code that calls child_process.execSync('echo <proof_string>') should return the proof string in the HTTP response, demonstrating arbitrary command execution on the server.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the vulnerable FUXA v1.2.7 repository, built the Docker image, started the FUXA server, waited for it to become ready, sent the crafted POST /api/runscript request without authentication headers, and received the proof string in the response confirming that child_process.execSync executed on the server.
verification_method: The exploit script generates a unique proof string (EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF_<pid>_<timestamp>), embeds it in a child_process.execSync('echo <proof>') call within the test script code, sends it to /api/runscript without any authentication token, and checks whether the proof string appears in the HTTP response body. This distinguishes real command execution from a generic error, startup failure, or unrelated output.
evidence_produced_by_run: The /api/runscript endpoint returned HTTP 200 with the proof string generated by child_process.execSync, confirming arbitrary OS command execution.
No x-access-token header was provided, confirming the exploit works without any authentication.
The FUXA server was in its default configuration with secureEnabled=false, matching real-world deployments.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit bbd738d21d7192d5eaaf58577de68eced4a609eb (v1.2.7), and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
